Petition For Surcharge Of Former Executor, Kelly Dixon
Off Calendar Feb-06-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McCaffrey for Rose Westerlund (Successor Executor).

Mr. McCaffrey stated that the issues with the accounting have been resolved, that they have a stipulation with regard to dividing of the statutory attorney''s fees and that he hopes to have the accounting filed by the end of the week. The Petition for Surcharge of Former Executor is granted (subject to determination of the amount of surcharge) and the report of the Successor Executor on Administration by Former Executor are approved (subject to receipt and review of the orders). The accounitng petition was continued to February 27, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Off Calendar Dec-01-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McCaffrey for Rose Westerlund.

The Court noted that Kelly Dixon was not present. Mr. McCaffrey stated that he would be able to proceed, that he just received some of the tax returns, that he could file further Franchise Tax Board waivers and file the accounting. Sanctions, The Order to Show Cause, and the Order of Status Hearing were taken off calendar. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Off Calendar Nov-15-2010 Off Calendar

Off Calendar Jul-12-2010 Continued To Nov-15-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

No appearance was made by Kelly Dixon. The Amended Account and response to the Examiner's notes have not been filed. Sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day continue to run. The Court will talley sanctions to date, reduce them to the form of an order and send notice to the State Bar. A body attachment for Kelly Dixon will be issued and bail will be set at $25,000.00.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Y. Watts.

MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to Kelly Dixon to appear on July 12, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why a body attachment should not be issued for failure to appear, for failure to file an amended account, for failure to file a response to the opposition and for failure to pay sanctions.

Off Calendar May-24-2010 Continued To Jun-21-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McCaffrey for Rose Westerlund.

The Court noted that Kelly Dixon was not present. Mr. McCaffrey stated that there was nothing to report, that he had no contact with Ms. Dixon and that he could file a report. The matters were continued to June 21, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of Ms. Dixon's Amended First and Final Account and for her response to the objections. Sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day continue to run. The Court waived Mr. McCaffrey's appearance at the next hearing.

Off Calendar Apr-26-2010 Continued To May-24-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McCaffrey for Rose Westerlund (Successor Executor). Kelly Dixon in Propria Persona.

The Court informed Ms. Dixon that sanctions were approaching the $3,000.00 mark. Ms. Dixon apologized to the Court and requested a continuance stating that she had been dealing with personal and family issues for the last six months. Mr. McCaffrey requested that Ms. Dixon turn over all records. The matter was continued to May 24, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. McCaffrey will provide Ms. Dixon with all of the First Republic Bank statements. The Amended First and Final Account is to be filed by Ms. Dixon by the end of business May 19, 2010.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-22-2010 Continued To Apr-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McCaffrey for Rose Westerlund.

Mr. McCaffrey stated that he had not heard from Kelly Dixon, that he was proceeding without her help, that he had the bank account, that he was not sure if Ms. Dixon had any financial statements and that he was waiting to see what happened to the Valero shares. The Order to Show Cause (OSC) was continued to April 26, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day will remain running. The OSC will include language as to why a body attachment for Ms. Dixon should not be issued for failure to appear, for failure to file the Amended Account and the response to Mr. McCaffrey's opposition. The account is to be brought current through January 31, 2010.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Sum.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-04-2010 Continued To Feb-22-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-04-2010 Continued To Feb-22-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McCaffrey for Rose Westerlund.

The Court inquired whether or not Mr. McCaffrey had received any documents from Ms. Dixon. Mr. McCaffrey stated that he had not received any documents from Ms. Dixon and noted that Ms. Navia didn't have any records to turn over to them. The Court changed the ending date of the first and final account period to be filed by Ms. Dixon from October 31, 2009 to November 30, 2009. In addition, the Court stated given Ms. Dixon's failure to appear and her failure to account that it was imposing sanctions of $25.00 per day against her beginning today until her accounting and her response to the objections are filed (order to be issued). The accounting and the Order to Show Cause are continued to February 22, 2010.

Judge Pro Tem: Sue Kaplan. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR# 11312. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-14-2009 Continued To Jan-04-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McCaffrey for Rose Westerlund.

The Court noted that Kelly Dixon was not present. An Order to Show Cause will be issued to Kelly Dixon to appear and show why she should not be held in contempt, surcharged or sanctioned for failure to file the Amended Account and for failure to file a response to the opposition. The return date is January 04, 2010 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-16-2009 Continued To Dec-14-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McCaffrey for Rose Westerlund, Pattie Sue Smith and Mary Bushma.

The Court noted that Kelly Dixon was not present. Mr. McCaffrey stated that Ms. Dixon had not responded, requested that she be removed and that Ms. Westerlund be appointed. The Court granted the petition removing Kelly Dixon as Executor and appointed Rose Westerlund as the Successor Executor to serve without bond, (subject to a written order). Ms. Dixon is to file an amended first and final account which is to be brought current and cover through October 31, 2009, and her response to the objections by November 25, 2009. The accounting petition was continued to December 14, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of the amended first and final account. Ms. Dixon is to turn over all property in her possession to the Successor Executor. Mr. McCaffrey will prepare the orders.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition To Remove Kelly Dixon As Executor, And For Suspension Of Her Powers, And For Appointment Of Successor Representative, And For Surcharge
Off Calendar Nov-19-2009 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-19-2009 Continued To Nov-16-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kelly Dixon (Executor) in Propria Persona. Brian McCaffrey for Reose Westerlund, Patty Sue Smith and Mary Catherine Bushma.

Ms. Dixon requested a continuance stating that the amended accounting petition was contingent upon getting information from Morgan Stanley, that it had been difficult due to medical issues and that she was waiting for the tax certification from the Franchise Tax Board. Mr. McCaffrey stated that this was the third time Ms. Dixon had failed to comply with the Court orders, that he wanted to renew his request to have Ms. Dixon removed and requested that no further extensions be granted. The matter was continued to November 16, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Dixon is to file the amended accounting petition no later than Friday October 30, 2009 and is to address Mr. McCaffrey's objections in writing. Mr. McCaffrey is to file an Amended Petition To Remove Kelly Dixon as Executor setting forth who would be appointed as Successor Executor. Said petition was also continued to November 16, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court will not address Ms. Dixon's request for preliminary distributions at this time.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition To Remove Kelly Dixon As Executor, And For Suspension Of Her Powers, And For Appointment Of Successor Representative, And For Surcharge
Off Calendar Oct-19-2009 Continued To Nov-16-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-31-2009 Continued To Oct-19-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for and with Kelly Dixon (Executor). Brian McCaffrey for Rose Westerlund, Pattie Sue Smith and Mary Bushma.

Ms. Navia stated that as of Friday, Ms. Dixon agreed to substitute in as Pro Per and that she would be filing the Substitution of Attorney today. In addition, Ms. Navia stated that the Accountant had not reported on the status of the Tax Certificate, that the missing Receipt on Preliminary Distribution would be filed by the next hearing and that her firm would file a seperate declaration regarding their fees. Mr. McCaffrey stated that the document submitted by Ms. Dixon was incomplete, that Ms. Dixon neglected the estate and that he wanted to renew his request to have Ms. Dixon removed. Ms. Dixon stated that the procedural issues raised by the Examiner had been taken care of, that she was working with the Accountant with regard to the Tax Certificate, that she was also working on the schedule for the Morgan Stanley account, and that the accounting needed to be revised and amended. In addition, Ms Dixon stated that she would be requesting extra ordinary fees, confirmed her mailing address, phone number and fax number, and agreed to accept service of the citation on the petition for removal at the Sutter Street address. Status on the amended accounting petition was set for October 19, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The petition to remove Executor was also continued to October 19, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court directed Ms. Dixon to address Mr. McCaffrey's objections and to address the issues raised by the Examiner if the issues have not been cured.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Maria Lertora, CSR#11935. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Off Calendar Jun-01-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for Kelly Dixon (Executor). Brian McCaffrey for Rose Westerlund, Pattie Sue Smith, and Mary Catherine Bushma (Beneficiaries).

Ms. Navia stated that the petition for the first and final account had been filed and that it was currently set for August 17, 2009. In addition, she stated that she wished to take her motion to withdraw as counsel off calendar; she would like to retain the option to raise it in the future if necessary. The Court granted Ms. Navia's request to take her motion to withdraw as counsel currently set for July 6, 2009 off calendar and noted if it was raised in the future she would be required to give notice. Mr. McCaffrey stated that he would need time to review the first and final account since he was just handed a copy of it today. Ms. Navia stated that if there are any issues with it she would like to have time to amend.
